question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted Transaction protobuf that sets both signedTransactionBytes and bodyBytes to non-empty values, causing parseTransaction() to silently prefer signedTransactionBytes and ignore bodyBytes, leading to a discrepancy between the parsed TransactionBody and the actual bytes stored, resulting in incorrect transaction attribution or data integrity violations?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Transaction where signedTransactionBytes deserializes to a valid SignedTransaction but the embedded bodyBytes within it deserializes to an empty TransactionBody (all default values), bypassing the null/empty check at line 430 and causing a RecordItem to be built with a zero-value payerAccountId and transactionType, leading to incorrect entity attribution in downstream processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a Transaction using the deprecated transaction.getBody() path (hasBody() == true) with a crafted TransactionBody that omits the TransactionID field, causing payerAccountId to be set to EntityId.of(default AccountID) (i.e., 0.0.0), which may collide with system accounts and corrupt entity transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TransactionBody with exactly one unknown protobuf field number that collides with a future or reserved transaction type, causing parseTransactionType() to return that field number as the transactionType, leading to misclassification of the transaction and bypassing type-specific processing logic in downstream handlers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionBody with DATA_NOT_SET and exactly one unknown field containing a very large integer field number, causing parseTransactionType() to return that large integer as transactionType, which may cause array index out of bounds or unexpected behavior in TransactionType.of() lookups downstream?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TransactionBody with DATA_NOT_SET and exactly one unknown field, causing parseTransactionType() to log a WARN message containing the raw field number and also log the full hex-encoded transaction body bytes via Hex.encodeHexString(body.toByteArray()) when unknownFields.size() != 1, potentially enabling log injection or log flooding that degrades monitoring and alerting systems?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionBody with DATA_NOT_SET and multiple unknown fields, causing parseTransactionType() to call log.error() with Hex.encodeHexString(body.toByteArray()) on the full body bytes, enabling an attacker to flood error logs with arbitrarily large hex-encoded payloads, causing disk exhaustion or log pipeline saturation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a child transaction record whose parentConsensusTimestamp matches previous.parent.transactionRecord.getParentConsensusTimestamp() (the grandparent path at line 340-344), causing parseParent() to return previous.parent.parent which may be null, resulting in a NullPointerException or incorrect null parent assignment that skips parent-success-propagation in parseSuccess()?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction record with a parentConsensusTimestamp that does not match any of the three checked timestamps (previous, previous.parent, previous.parent's parent), causing parseParent() to fall through and return the builder-supplied this.parent (which may be null or stale), leading to incorrect parent-child linkage and wrong success propagation via parseSuccess()?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a batch of transactions where a child's parentConsensusTimestamp is set to match previous.parent.transactionRecord.getConsensusTimestamp() but previous.parent is itself a failed transaction, causing parseSuccess() to return false for the child even though the child's own receipt shows SUCCESS, leading to suppression of legitimate successful child transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a long chain of previous RecordItems all having non-null ContractFunctionResults with contractNum != 365, causing the while loop in parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to traverse the entire chain without finding HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM, resulting in O(n) traversal that degrades parsing performance proportional to the chain length?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction record with a ContractFunctionResult whose contractNum equals the hardcoded HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM (365) but is not actually a hook contract, causing parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent() to incorrectly assign hookParent, leading to wrong log index attribution and incorrect contractRelatedParent resolution in SyntheticContractLogServiceImpl?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a child transaction with a ContractFunctionResult where contractNum == HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM (365) at the current item level, causing the condition currentTxnRecordContractResult.getContractID().getContractNum() != HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM to be false and skipping hookParent resolution entirely, allowing a genuine hook child to be misclassified as a non-hook transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isSystemFileUpdate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FileUpdate transaction body and submit it with payerAccountId set to account 0.0.50 (shard=0, realm=0, num=50), causing isSystemFileUpdate() to return true and isTopLevel() to return true for what is actually a child transaction (hasParentConsensusTimestamp == true), bypassing child-transaction processing restrictions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isTopLevel()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction record with transactionNonce > 0 and scheduled == true, causing isTopLevel() to return true via the second condition at line 226, even if the transaction has a parentConsensusTimestamp set, leading to incorrect top-level classification and double-processing of scheduled child transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isTopLevel()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction record with transactionNonce == 0 and hasParentConsensusTimestamp == true, causing isTopLevel() to return true (because nonce==0 short-circuits), even though the transaction is semantically a child, leading to incorrect top-level processing and potential duplication of entity or contract transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionRecord whose transactionHash ByteString is shorter than 32 bytes, causing Arrays.copyOfRange(DomainUtils.toBytes(...), 0, 32) to pad the result with zero bytes, producing a hash collision with other transactions that have a hash prefix matching the short hash, leading to incorrect transaction deduplication or lookup?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionRecord with an empty transactionHash ByteString, causing DomainUtils.toBytes() to return an empty or null byte array, and Arrays.copyOfRange(..., 0, 32) to return a 32-byte zero-filled array, resulting in all such transactions sharing the same hash value (0x000...000) and breaking hash-based lookups in the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user set an EthereumTransaction on a RecordItem (via setEthereumTransaction()) with a null or empty hash, causing getTransactionHash() to return null or an empty byte array instead of falling back to the record's transactionHash, leading to NullPointerExceptions in downstream consumers like ContractResultServiceImpl that use the hash for ContractLog entries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger concurrent processing of a single RecordItem (e.g., via parallel sidecar processing), causing a race condition in getAndIncrementLogIndex() where the null check `if (logIndex == null)` and the subsequent `logIndex = new AtomicInteger(0)` are not synchronized, resulting in two threads both seeing null and both initializing logIndex, causing one thread's initialization to be lost and log indices to start from 0 twice, producing duplicate log index values?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where getAndIncrementLogIndex() is called on a child RecordItem that delegates log index management to a parent via getContractRelatedParent(), but the child also independently calls getAndIncrementLogIndex() directly, causing log index sequences to diverge between parent and child and producing non-sequential log indices in the database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that causes addContractTransaction() to be called with an EntityId whose getId() returns a value that collides with an existing key in the contractTransactions HashMap, causing computeIfAbsent() to silently skip insertion and the second entity's contract transaction to never be recorded, leading to missing contract transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where contractTransactionPredicate is set to null (via setContractTransactionPredicate(null)), causing addContractTransaction() to return early at the null check on line 140 and never record any contract transactions, effectively suppressing all contract transaction tracking for that RecordItem without any error or warning?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger concurrent calls to addContractTransaction() on the same RecordItem, causing a race condition in getContractTransactions() where the null check `if (contractTransactions == null)` and `contractTransactions = new HashMap<>()` are not synchronized, resulting in two threads creating separate HashMaps and one being discarded, causing contract transaction entries to be lost?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: '] [Function: populateContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger populateContractTransactions() to be called multiple times on the same RecordItem, causing contractTransaction.setContractIds(ids) to be called repeatedly with the same list, and since ids is derived from contractTransactions.keySet() which is a live view, subsequent calls may produce inconsistent contractIds lists if the map is modified between calls, leading to data corruption in persisted ContractTransaction records?
